key_insight: CANN-EUCLID discovers sparse, interpretable hyperelastic material laws directly from full-field displacement and reaction-force data without local stress measurements.
engineering_relevance: This enables constitutive model identification from a single heterogeneous test, which is especially valuable for soft biological tissues where repeated homogeneous testing is costly or damaging.
method: Combine constitutive neural networks (CANNs) with the EUCLID stress-unsupervised full-field discovery framework
Minimize equilibrium imbalance while using sparsity-promoting regularization to select a compact set of active constitutive terms
Recover isotropic and anisotropic hyperelastic laws from displacement fields and reaction forces in one loading case
evidence_signal: Across isotropic and anisotropic benchmarks, the method recovered ground-truth terms nearly exactly when they were in the basis and validated the discovered laws through forward finite-element simulations.
watch_item: Recovery and extrapolation depend strongly on the sampled deformation range, so missing stiffening regimes can lead to large prediction errors outside the observed domain.
paper_title: CANN-EUCLID: unsupervised constitutive artificial neural network model discovery from full-field data
